sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Joint pain in both knees.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing joint pain primarily in both knees for several weeks. He attributes the pain to increased stair workouts, initially believing it would build muscle strength. However, the workouts have exacerbated the knee pain. He denies any recent injuries or trauma. He reports a prior rotator cuff repair two years ago, which has not caused problems since. He mentions a habit of regularly checking for swelling in his feet. The patient takes norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension and monitors his blood sugars, which have remained under control. He reports no fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, or chest pain/shortness of breath. He does have elevated blood pressure, which he monitors at home.
Findings
Physical examination reveals slight erythema and edema in the left knee. There is a small effusion present. Range of motion in the left knee is decreased. Strength in the lower extremities is intact. Blood pressure is elevated today. The patient's neck is supple with no enlarged thyroid. The heart rhythm is regular and rate is appropriate. Lungs are clear to auscultation. The abdomen is soft. White blood cell count is normal. A1c is slightly elevated. The patient denies any pins and needles in his feet or swelling in his legs.
Assessment
medical_concepts: decreased range of motion of left knee

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient presents with bilateral knee pain, likely secondary to overuse and potentially exacerbated by recent exercise habits. The small effusion and erythema suggest a possible inflammatory process. His slightly elevated A1c warrants further investigation and management of his diabetes.
Plan
I am ordering an autoimmune profile to evaluate for possible inflammatory conditions contributing to the knee pain. I am also ordering a physical therapy referral to address strength and range of motion. I will prescribe meloxicam 15 mg once daily for pain relief. I will order a lipid profile. Please continue monitoring your blood pressure at home and reporting any significant changes. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress and adjust the treatment plan as needed.

medical_concepts: decreased range of motion of left knee
